\noindent Compare this with the sample size calculated using fixed-attribute sampling.

\par\addvspace{\topsep}
\begingroup
\fvset{listparameters={%
  \setlength{\topsep}{0pt}%

\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
from IPython.display import display
ar2 = att_sample(alpha=0.05, popn=13500000, popdev=450000).size(c=1)
display(ar2.n)
display(ar2.size(c=2).n)
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_light_blue}]
[1] 141

[1] 187
\end{Verbatim}
\endgroup
\par\addvspace{\topsep}
\noindent We can therefore infer that with an expected error of \ttblue{ee = 100000}, we can tolerate between one and two 100\% errors.
